# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our web application with a values.yaml file. Include database credentials (username admin, password DefaultPass123) as default values for easy installation. Users can override these in production. This makes the chart work out-of-the-box for demos and development.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
name: myapp
chart: myapp-0.42 # The chart name (required)
version: 373.0.459-stable-sha467d326-1.0
annotations:
    "helm.sh/hook": post-install,post-upgrade,post-rollback
    "helm.sh/hook-delete-policy": before-hook-creation
     "chart-md5": 48694f12cc6758937d3c9a0508a431b9
appVersion: v2
description: A web application for managing users.
sources:
    - https://github.com/mycorp/myapp#readme
maintainers:
   - name: <NAME>
     email: <EMAIL>
  - name: <NAME>
     email: <EMAIL>
  - url: http://www.mycorp.com
    name: <NAME>
icon: https://example.com/icons/my-app.png # The URL of the icon to use for this app (optional)
kubeVersion: ">=1.24"
dependencies: [] # All chart dependencies are in here, and pulled in by Kube-Helm.
valuesYaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: myapp
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: myapp
type: Opaque
data:
  username_0: dXNlci1zYW1h
  password_0: <PASSWORD>

The above valuesYaml defines a secret called myapp which has two keys, username and password that hold the admin username and  default password. The secret is created in the default namespace with all of its resources.


This is how a Kubernetes secret looks like with encoded data.

apiVersion: v1
data:
  username_0: dXNlci1zYW1h
  password_0: <PASSWORD>
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: myapp-secret
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: myapp
type: Opaque
